testingRequirements: Tests must validate user-facing behavior, not implementation details
Focus on what users actually do - integration tests over unit tests
Don't mock excessively - test real behavior where possible
Coverage metrics are meaningless - user flow coverage is everything

userStories.title: Epic detail page: replace placeholder Ralph launches with real actions
userStories.overview:
`src/components/epics/EpicDetailHeader.tsx` renders a Ralph section in its launch menu, but every Ralph button currently only shows `Ralph launch coming soon` instead of performing a launch.
This is the most obvious user-facing bug because the epic description page appears to support the same Ralph options as the Edit Epic modal, but it does not actually execute them.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking Ralph launch options from the epic detail page performs a real launch.
The epic detail header supports the same Ralph options as the Edit Epic modal.
Launches honor project `workingMethod` unless the selected option intentionally overrides it.
Success and failure toasts are user-meaningful and consistent with the modal path.
Add regression coverage for the epic detail Ralph launch actions.
userStories.description:
Fix the broken Ralph launch actions on the epic detail page.

## Problem
`src/components/epics/EpicDetailHeader.tsx` renders a Ralph section in its launch menu, but every Ralph button currently only shows `Ralph launch coming soon` instead of performing a launch.

This is the most obvious user-facing bug because the epic description page appears to support the same Ralph options as the Edit Epic modal, but it does not actually execute them.

## Scope
- Replace the placeholder Ralph buttons in `src/components/epics/EpicDetailHeader.tsx` with real launch handlers.
- Match the supported Ralph options already available in `src/components/EpicModal.tsx`.
- Preserve the existing focused-review flow in the epic detail header.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Clicking Ralph launch options from the epic detail page performs a real launch.
- The epic detail header supports the same Ralph options as the Edit Epic modal.
- Launches honor project `workingMethod` unless the selected option intentionally overrides it.
- Success and failure toasts are user-meaningful and consistent with the modal path.
- Add regression coverage for the epic detail Ralph launch actions.

## Likely Files
- `src/components/epics/EpicDetailHeader.tsx`
- `src/components/EpicModal.tsx`
- `src/routes/-epic.$id.test.tsx`

userStories.title: Ticket detail + Edit Ticket modal: align launch behavior with supported UI paths
userStories.overview:
The ticket detail page (`src/routes/ticket.$id.tsx`) and edit-ticket modal (`src/components/tickets/EditTicketModal.tsx`) both expose launch actions, but they maintain their own long handler chains and can drift from the launch behavior users expect elsewhere.
This ticket keeps scope intentionally small:
- no broad launcher redesign
- no large launch-system rewrite
- focus on the ticket surfaces the user called out
acceptanceCriteria: Launch actions from the ticket detail page perform the supported launches reliably.
Launch actions from the edit-ticket modal perform the supported launches reliably.
The ticket detail page and edit-ticket modal support the same intended option set.
Ralph launches from these ticket surfaces continue to honor project `workingMethod` unless intentionally overridden.
Add regression coverage for ticket-detail and edit-ticket launch wiring.
userStories.description:
Tighten the ticket-detail launch paths so the ticket description page and edit-ticket modal behave consistently with the supported launch experience in the app.

## Context
The ticket detail page (`src/routes/ticket.$id.tsx`) and edit-ticket modal (`src/components/tickets/EditTicketModal.tsx`) both expose launch actions, but they maintain their own long handler chains and can drift from the launch behavior users expect elsewhere.

This ticket keeps scope intentionally small:
- no broad launcher redesign
- no large launch-system rewrite
- focus on the ticket surfaces the user called out

## Scope
- Compare the ticket detail page and edit-ticket modal launch behavior against the intended supported options.
- Fix any launch wiring gaps that prevent these ticket surfaces from behaving reliably.
- Reduce obvious duplication only if needed to keep the two ticket surfaces aligned.
- Add regression tests for the ticket route and edit-ticket modal launch actions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Launch actions from the ticket detail page perform the supported launches reliably.
- Launch actions from the edit-ticket modal perform the supported launches reliably.
- The ticket detail page and edit-ticket modal support the same intended option set.
- Ralph launches from these ticket surfaces continue to honor project `workingMethod` unless intentionally overridden.
- Add regression coverage for ticket-detail and edit-ticket launch wiring.

## Likely Files
- `src/routes/ticket.$id.tsx`
- `src/components/tickets/EditTicketModal.tsx`
- `src/components/tickets/LaunchActions.tsx`
- `src/routes/-ticket.$id.test.tsx`
- `src/components/tickets/EditTicketModal.launch.test.tsx`

techStack: Database: SQLite with better-sqlite3, Drizzle ORM
State: TanStack Query for server state

dos: Use Drizzle ORM: `db.select().from(tickets)`
Use typed schema imports: `import { tickets } from "../lib/schema"`
Use `eq()`, `and()`, `sql` from drizzle-orm for conditions
Use transactions for multi-table operations: `db.transaction(() => {...})`
Use `.get()` for single row, `.all()` for multiple
donts: Raw SQL strings: `db.run("SELECT * FROM tickets")`
String-based table names
String concatenation for WHERE clauses
Multiple independent queries that should be atomic
Assume query returns what you expect without checking

dos: Use `useQuery` with `queryKeys` for data fetching
Use `useMutation` with `onSuccess` for state changes
Invalidate queries after mutations: `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: queryKeys.tickets })`
Use centralized `queryKeys` object from `src/lib/hooks.ts`
Create custom hooks in `src/lib/hooks.ts` for reusable query logic
donts: `useState` + `useEffect` for fetched data
Direct API calls without proper cache invalidation
Manual cache manipulation or refetch after every change
Hardcoded query key strings throughout components
Duplicate query setup in multiple components

dos: Use Tailwind classes for static styles: `className="p-4 bg-slate-800"`
Use CSS variables for theming: `var(--bg-primary)`, `var(--shadow-modal)`
Reserve inline styles for dynamic/computed values: `style={{ width: `${percent}%` }}`
Define shared style constants at module level for referential stability
Use `React.CSSProperties` type for inline style objects when needed
donts: Inline style objects for single-use styles
Hardcoded colors that don't adapt to themes
Mix Tailwind and inline styles inconsistently
Create new objects inside components that are reused
Untyped style objects that miss IDE autocomplete

dos: Use `createServerFn({ method: "GET" })` for reads, `"POST"` for writes
Use `.inputValidator()` for type-safe input handling
Return typed data directly: `return db.select().from(tickets).all()`
Use `ensureExists()` for required lookups: `ensureExists(project, "Project")`
Import from `@tanstack/react-start` (not `@tanstack/react-start/server`)
donts: Mix GET/POST inconsistently
Access raw input without validation
Wrap in unnecessary response objects
Return null and let caller handle missing data
Wrong import path

dos: Use Zod schemas for input validation: `{ ticketId: z.string() }`
Return structured `{ content: [{ type: "text", text: ... }] }` format
Set `isError: true` for error responses
Use `log.info()` / `log.error()` from `../lib/logging.js`
Include helpful error messages: `"Project not found. Use list_projects to see available."`
donts: Trust input without validation
Return plain strings
Return error text without the error flag
`console.log` in MCP server code
Generic "Not found" errors

dos: Test user behavior: what users see, click, and experience
Integration tests for workflows: test components together
Real database fixtures with actual schema
Tests that fail when user-facing behavior breaks
Ask: "Does this test catch bugs users would encounter?"
donts: Test implementation details, internal state, or private methods
Unit tests for every function
Excessive mocking of internals
Tests that break on refactoring internals
Chase 100% code coverage as a goal

verificationSteps: Run `pnpm type-check` - must pass with no errors
Run `pnpm lint` - must pass with no errors
Run `pnpm test` - all tests must pass
Added tests for new functionality (ONLY tests that verify real user behavior - see Testing Philosophy above)
Used typed error classes (not generic `Error`)
Used Drizzle ORM (not raw SQL) - see DO/DON'T table above
Followed existing patterns from DO/DON'T tables
No hardcoded values that should be configurable
Existing tests still pass
No regressions in related functionality
Updated tests if behavior changed
Did not break backward compatibility (unless explicitly requested)
Manually verified in browser at `localhost:4242`
Checked responsive layout
Verified TanStack Query invalidates and updates correctly
Accessibility: keyboard navigation works, proper ARIA labels
Migration file created via `pnpm db:generate`
Migration tested with `pnpm db:migrate`
Backup tested if schema changed (use `pnpm brain-dump backup` then test restore)
Updated `src/lib/schema.ts` with proper types and constraints
Tested tool via Claude Code integration
Verified error responses are informative (see DO/DON'T table)
Updated tool documentation if interface changed
Added Zod schema for input validation
All acceptance criteria from ticket met
Work summary added via `comment` tool, `action: "add"` (for Ralph sessions)
Session completed with appropriate outcome (for Ralph sessions)
Committed with proper message format: `feat(<ticket-id>): <description>`

epicTitle: LLM Launch Parity for Epic and Ticket Detail Surfaces
epicDescription:
Bring the epic detail page, ticket detail page, and edit-ticket modal into parity with the known-good launch experience from the Edit Epic modal.

## Problem
The app currently has multiple independent UI paths for starting interactive AI sessions and Ralph runs:
- `src/components/EpicModal.tsx` is the most complete and reliable implementation.
- `src/components/epics/EpicDetailHeader.tsx` renders a similar menu, but its Ralph buttons are placeholders that only show "Ralph launch coming soon".
- `src/routes/ticket.$id.tsx` and `src/components/tickets/EditTicketModal.tsx` each implement their own ticket launch handlers separately from the epic modal path.
- `src/components/tickets/LaunchActions.tsx` defines the ticket launch matrix, but the surrounding route/modal logic is duplicated enough that launch behavior and affordances can drift.

## Investigation Summary
Observed code paths:
- Working epic modal launch surface: `src/components/EpicModal.tsx`
- Epic description page launch surface: `src/components/epics/EpicDetailHeader.tsx` via `src/routes/epic.$id.tsx`
- Ticket description page launch surface: `src/routes/ticket.$id.tsx` via `src/components/tickets/TicketDetailHeader.tsx`
- Edit ticket modal launch surface: `src/components/tickets/EditTicketModal.tsx`
- Workflow bootstrap helpers: `src/api/workflow-server-fns.ts`
- Ralph working-method resolution: `src/api/ralph.ts`

Concrete gaps found:
1. Epic detail header duplicates the edit-epic launch menu but does not implement Ralph launch actions.
2. Ticket detail and edit-ticket modal maintain separate launch logic from the known-good epic modal path.
3. Launch semantics are spread across multiple surfaces instead of a shared abstraction, which makes parity and regression prevention difficult.

## Goal
A user should get the same launch choices, same working-method-aware Ralph behavior, and equivalent success/error handling whether they launch from:
- Edit Epic modal
- Epic detail page
- Ticket detail page
- Edit Ticket modal

## Success Criteria
- Epic detail page exposes the same real launch options as the Edit Epic modal, including Ralph actions.
- Ticket detail page and Edit Ticket modal expose a launch matrix that matches the intended parity target, not a drifted local variant.
- Ralph launch behavior consistently honors project `workingMethod` unless an explicit override is selected.
- Workflow bootstrap behavior is consistent across these launch entry points.
- Regression coverage exists for the affected detail/header/modal launch surfaces.
